key_insight: The paper makes cell-wise (element-local) vectors the persistent data model and shows that, with a continuous-image preconditioner, flexible CG and matrix-free FEM can be carried out exactly without assembling global vectors.
engineering_relevance: This enables GPU-friendly high-order finite elements with reduced synchronization and communication overhead, while preserving correctness of Krylov iteration and improving performance.
method: Uses a primal-dual pairing identity to match local Krylov scalars to assembled-solve values.
Implements DSS via dimensionally split face-exchange sweeps instead of gather-scatter, atomics, or coloring.
Handles unstructured macro-block interfaces and h-adaptive hanging nodes with separate topological kernels and a shadow-cell wrapper.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on modern GPUs showing operator evaluation and solver throughput outperform state-of-the-art matrix-free implementations.
watch_item: Performance depends on the preconditioner and on managing layout choices, so blocked storage and topology handling may add implementation complexity.
paper_title: Coalesced Matrix-Free Finite Elements in Cell-Wise Storage
